concepts.subject: Physics
concepts.summary: Kinematics is the branch of mechanics that describes how objects move — without worrying about the forces that cause the motion. It deals with position, displacement, velocity, and acceleration.
common_mistakes: Forgetting that 'g' is negative when an object moves upward
Using wrong sign convention for displacement vs distance

worked_example.problem: A ball is thrown upward with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. How high does it rise? (g = 10 m/s²)
steps: At the highest point, final velocity v = 0
Use v² = u² − 2gs (since g acts downward when motion is upward)

concepts.subject: Mathematics
concepts.summary: Trigonometric identities are equations involving trig functions that are true for every value of the variable. The 'big three' are the Pythagorean identity, the sum/difference formulas, and the double-angle formulas.

key_formulas.label: Sum
key_formulas.formula: sin(A+B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B

common_mistakes: Forgetting that sin²θ means (sin θ)², not sin(θ²)
Mixing up cos(A+B) and cos A + cos B — they're not the same!
Dropping signs when working in non-standard quadrants

worked_example.problem: Prove that (1 − cos 2θ) / sin 2θ = tan θ
steps: Use double-angle formulas: 1 − cos 2θ = 2 sin²θ and sin 2θ = 2 sin θ cos θ
Substitute: (2 sin²θ) / (2 sin θ cos θ)
Simplify: sin θ / cos θ = tan θ ✓

concepts.subject: Chemistry
concepts.summary: Chemical bonds are the forces that hold atoms together. The two main types are ionic bonds (electron transfer) and covalent bonds (electron sharing). The bond formed depends on the electronegativity difference between atoms.

key_formulas.label: Bond order
key_formulas.formula: BO = ½ (Nb − Na), where Nb = bonding electrons, Na = antibonding

key_formulas.label: Electronegativity Δ
key_formulas.formula: Ionic if Δ > 1.7, polar covalent if 0.4 < Δ < 1.7, nonpolar if Δ < 0.4

common_mistakes: Confusing bond order with oxidation number
Forgetting lone pairs when assigning formal charges
Assuming all C-H bonds are nonpolar (they're slightly polar)

worked_example.problem: Predict the bond type in NaCl (ENNa = 0.9, ENCl = 3.0)
steps: Since Δ > 1.7, the bond is ionic
Na donates an electron to Cl, forming Na⁺ and Cl⁻

concepts.subject: Biology
concepts.summary: Cellular respiration is how cells break down glucose to release energy stored as ATP. It happens in three main stages: glycolysis (cytoplasm), Krebs cycle (mitochondrial matrix), and oxidative phosphorylation (inner mitochondrial membrane).
common_mistakes: Saying respiration only happens in mitochondria — glycolysis is in the cytoplasm
Forgetting that fermentation is anaerobic and only yields 2 ATP
Confusing respiration with breathing (respiration is cellular)

worked_example.problem: Why is aerobic respiration more efficient than anaerobic?
steps: Anaerobic respiration (fermentation) stops at pyruvate → lactate or ethanol, yielding only 2 ATP
Aerobic respiration continues through Krebs cycle and ETC, fully oxidising glucose
Aerobic yields ~30 ATP vs 2 ATP — a 15× difference
worked_example.answer: Aerobic respiration extracts ~15× more energy per glucose molecule.

concepts.subject: Computer Science
concepts.summary: Python has two main loop types: 'for' loops (iterate over a sequence) and 'while' loops (run while a condition is True). Use 'for' when you know how many iterations; use 'while' when you don't.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to update the loop variable in a while loop → infinite loop
Using range(1, n) when you mean range(n) — off-by-one error
Mutating a list while iterating over it

worked_example.problem: Print numbers 1 to 5 using a for loop

study_techniques.name: Active recall
study_techniques.description: Test yourself instead of re-reading. Cover the page and try to write the answer from memory.

study_techniques.name: Spaced repetition
study_techniques.description: Review material at increasing intervals: 1 day, 3 days, 1 week, 2 weeks.

study_techniques.name: Feynman technique
study_techniques.description: Explain the concept in simple words as if teaching a child. If you get stuck, you've found a gap.

study_techniques.name: Pomodoro technique
study_techniques.description: Study in focused 25-minute blocks with 5-minute breaks. After 4 blocks, take a longer 15-min break.

study_techniques.name: Interleaving
study_techniques.description: Mix different topics or subjects in one study session instead of blocking one topic at a time.
